# Re-engage upcoming appointments with PostgreSQL and Discord

**Pattern:** Reminders & Follow-ups ¬∑ **Level:** Production ¬∑ **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Re-engage upcoming appointments with PostgreSQL and Discord. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual drip campaigns is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule ‚Üí Stamp & Shape Email ‚Üí Payload Valid? ‚Üí Slot Free? ‚Üí Record Booking ‚Üí Send Confirmation (Discord) ‚Üí Hold Until Reminder Time ‚Üí ‚Ä¶

## Use it for
- Teams that want to reminders & follow-ups without manual effort
- Anyone automating drip campaigns in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Responds in seconds
- Scales without extra headcount

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Discord webhook URL; Google OAuth2 / service account; Postgres connection; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: üü¶ entry ¬∑ üü™ AI ¬∑ üü´ actions ¬∑ ‚¨ú utilities ¬∑ üü© outputs ¬∑ üü• error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath ‚Äî read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger ‚Äî hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click ‚Üí Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Availability is checked *before* booking, not assumed ‚Äî creating an event unconditionally would double-book the same slot if two requests arrive close together. The confirmation and the later reminder are separate sends because they serve different moments: confirming intent now, and cutting no-shows right before the appointment.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade ‚Äî deepen your understanding:

1. Change the reminder lead time from 1 hour to 24 hours.
2. Simulate two near-simultaneous booking requests for the same slot and see which gets the alternative-slots branch.
3. Add a cancellation path that frees the slot back up.

## üõü Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3√ó with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail ‚Üí context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ‚è± Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait ‚Äî throughput drops, reliability rises.

## üßπ Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node ‚Üí 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## üõÝ Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see üîë lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your drip campaigns process. Every node card explains its role ‚Äî safe to extend at any marked branch.
